hoppscotch.name: Hoppscotch
hoppscotch.short_description: Open-source API development ecosystem that helps you build, test, and document HTTP and GraphQL requests faster.

features.description: Supports testing multiple API protocol types in one interface.
features.title: REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, and SSE testing

features.description: Can be self-hosted instead of relying on the hosted cloud version.
features.title: Self-hostable deployment

features.description: Supports collaborating on API collections in real time.
features.title: Real-time collaboration

pros: Open source and self-hostable, unlike most API-testing tools
Supports REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, and SSE in one tool

faqs.answer: Yes — Hoppscotch is open source, with a hosted version and a self-hostable version both available.
faqs.question: Is Hoppscotch free to use?

mockserver.name: MockServer
mockserver.short_description: MockServer is a free, open-source tool to mock, proxy, and verify HTTP, HTTPS, gRPC, and GraphQL APIs for testing.

features.description: Return controllable, dynamic responses for any request to isolate services under test.
features.title: Mocking

features.description: Record, replay, and inject chaos into real traffic passing through a proxy.
features.title: Proxying

features.description: Assert which requests were sent, using MockServer as part of test assertions.
features.title: Verification

features.description: Inject latency, errors, dropped connections, and malformed responses to test resilience.
features.title: Chaos Testing

features.description: Supports HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, HTTPS, gRPC, AsyncAPI messaging, GraphQL, and OAuth2/OIDC.
features.title: Broad Protocol Support

features.description: Mocks OpenAI, Anthropic, and Bedrock-style APIs, including token streaming.
features.title: LLM Mocking

features.description: Set breakpoints to pause and modify requests and responses live.
features.title: Interactive Debugging

features.description: Run built-in declarative load scenarios.
features.title: Load Testing

pros: Completely free and open source, no SaaS account required
Broad protocol support including HTTP/2, HTTP/3, gRPC, and GraphQL
Multiple deployment options: Docker, executable JAR, Helm/Kubernetes, Testcontainers, Homebrew
Client libraries for many languages: Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Go, .NET, Rust, PHP
Built-in chaos and load testing in addition to basic mocking
cons: No managed/hosted SaaS offering documented on the site — requires running and managing your own instance
No listed company, founding year, or dedicated commercial support plans
Documentation does not state an explicit license type

faqs.answer: Yes, MockServer is free and open-source with no SaaS account required.
faqs.question: Is MockServer free?

faqs.answer: Via Docker, an executable JAR, Homebrew, Kubernetes Helm charts, or Testcontainers.
faqs.question: How do I run MockServer?

faqs.answer: HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, HTTPS, gRPC, AsyncAPI messaging, GraphQL, and OAuth2/OIDC.
faqs.question: What protocols does MockServer support?

faqs.answer: Yes, it includes built-in mocking for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Bedrock-style APIs with token streaming.
faqs.question: Can MockServer mock AI/LLM APIs?

faqs.answer: Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Go, .NET, Rust, and PHP, plus a REST API and CLI.
faqs.question: Which languages have client libraries?

mezmo.name: Mezmo
mezmo.short_description: Telemetry pipeline platform that ingests, transforms, and routes logs, metrics, and traces, paired with an AI agent for root cause analysis.

features.description: Processes logs, metrics, and traces in real time before they reach downstream observability tools.
features.title: Active Telemetry pipeline

features.description: Enables real-time streaming and replay of telemetry data for incident investigation.
features.title: Live tail and replay

features.description: Captures critical errors while reducing the volume of routine telemetry data.
features.title: Tail-based sampling

features.description: Identifies high-volume, low-value data streams for optimization.
features.title: Data profiling

features.description: Supports defining telemetry pipelines as code for CI/CD integration.
features.title: Pipeline-as-code

features.description: Aggregates and manages metric cardinality to help control observability costs.
features.title: Cardinality management

features.description: An open-source agentic harness where AI agents investigate infrastructure incidents with human-in-the-loop review before risky actions are taken.
features.title: Agentic root cause analysis (AURA)

pros: Telemetry pipeline filters and transforms data before it reaches costly downstream observability tools, which one customer used to cut Datadog costs by 52%
AI root cause analysis agent is included in the platform license with no separate metered query charges
Pipeline-as-code support integrates with CI/CD workflows
Human-in-the-loop review keeps risky automated actions reviewable before they touch production
The AURA agentic component is open source
cons: Specific pricing tiers and a starting price are not published on the website
Configuring pipeline routing and transformation rules requires understanding telemetry pipeline concepts
Free access is limited to a trial rather than an ongoing free tier

faqs.answer: It is a real-time engine that ingests, transforms, and routes logs, metrics, and traces before they reach observability tools or storage.
faqs.question: What is Mezmo's Active Telemetry pipeline?

faqs.answer: No, according to Mezmo the AI root cause analysis agent is included in the platform license with no metered query charges.
faqs.question: Does Mezmo charge extra for AI features?

faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available directly on Mezmo's website.
faqs.question: Is there a free trial?

faqs.answer: AURA is Mezmo's open-source agentic harness that lets AI agents investigate and help resolve production infrastructure incidents with human oversight.
faqs.question: What is AURA?

speakeasy.name: Speakeasy
speakeasy.short_description: Speakeasy generates SDKs and CLIs from OpenAPI specs, and offers a separate AI control plane for governing AI agent access to company systems.
speakeasy.starting_price: Free (SDK & MCP generation, 250 operations/language); Business plan from $720/month ($600/month billed annually)

features.description: Generates client SDKs and Model Context Protocol servers from OpenAPI specs.
features.title: SDK & MCP Generation

features.description: Publishes generated SDKs automatically to a package manager.
features.title: Auto-Publish

features.description: Adds OAuth support, webhooks, and SSE to generated SDKs (Business plan).
features.title: OAuth & Webhooks

features.description: A control layer for connecting AI agents to SaaS tools, APIs, and internal systems with policy enforcement.
features.title: AI Control Plane

features.description: Blocks PII, secrets, and prompt-injection attempts in AI agent traffic.
features.title: Real-Time Inspection

features.description: Includes Salesforce, Slack, HubSpot, and identity providers such as Okta and Entra ID.
features.title: Pre-Built Integrations

features.description: Tracks AI agent usage by team, tool, and user with a full audit trail.
features.title: Usage Observability

pros: Free tier for SDK/MCP generation includes 250 operations per language and community Slack support.
Customers retain ownership of generated code even after cancelling a plan.
SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certified.
Supports MCP-based agents including Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot.
cons: Business plan is priced per licensed programming language, which adds up for multi-language SDK support.
The AI Control Plane product is Enterprise-only with 'tailored' pricing that is not public.
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site.

faqs.answer: Yes, the SDK & CLI free tier includes SDK/MCP generation with 250 operations per language.
faqs.question: Does Speakeasy have a free plan?

faqs.answer: $720/month per licensed language, or $600/month billed annually, with a free trial available.
faqs.question: What does the Business plan cost?

faqs.answer: Speakeasy states customers own 100% of the code already generated, though new code can no longer be generated without an active plan.
faqs.question: What happens to generated SDK code if I cancel?

faqs.answer: Yes, it supports Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and other MCP-compatible agents.
faqs.question: Does Speakeasy support MCP-based AI agents?

errbit.name: Errbit

features.description: Existing Airbrake clients can point their airbrake gem or SDK at an Errbit instance with no code changes.

Available as a Docker image for easier deployment
cons: Requires self-hosting and infrastructure maintenance with no managed cloud option

httpie.name: HTTPie
httpie.short_description: HTTPie is an open-source API testing toolset with a CLI, a cross-platform desktop app, and a web app.

features.description: The original open-source command-line tool, installable via apt, brew, choco, pip, port, snap, yum, and more; installs both `http` and `https` commands.
features.title: Terminal/CLI

features.description: Expressive and intuitive command syntax with first-class JSON support and colorized, formatted terminal output.
features.title: Expressive syntax

features.description: A graphical, cross-platform desktop application (Mac, Windows, Linux) currently in public beta, offering the CLI's functionality in a GUI.
features.title: Desktop app

features.description: A browser-based version of HTTPie accessible without installing anything.
features.title: Web app

features.description: The Desktop app advertises the ability to 'leverage the power of AI-assisted work' alongside a dedicated HTTPie AI product.
features.title: AI-assisted work

features.description: Spaces, collections, and tabs for organizing requests, plus variables and environments for request parametrization.
features.title: Organization tools

features.description: The Desktop app can be used offline and without creating an account, with cross-device sync and auto-save when signed in.
features.title: Offline & account-optional

features.description: Persistent sessions, forms and file uploads, HTTPS/proxy/authentication support, a wget-like download mode, and an extensions API for authentication.
features.title: Sessions and auth

pros: CLI is free, open source, and has over 47,900 GitHub stars
Available across CLI, desktop, and web, so teams can pick the interface that fits their workflow
Desktop app works offline and doesn't require an account
Simple, human-readable request syntax with strong JSON support
Backed by $6.5M seed funding from Coatue, Blossom Capital, and founders of Intercom, Checkout.com, and Monzo
cons: The Desktop app is still in public beta
No public pricing page was found, so cost of any paid tiers (if they exist) is unclear
Detailed documentation on integrations and enterprise/team features is not surfaced on the marketing site

faqs.answer: The HTTPie CLI is free and open source. The Desktop app can also be used offline and without an account; no public pricing page currently lists paid plans.
faqs.question: Is HTTPie free?

faqs.answer: HTTPie Desktop is described as cross-platform, supporting Mac, Windows, and Linux.
faqs.question: What platforms does HTTPie Desktop support?

faqs.answer: Yes, the CLI is open source, with the project describing itself as 'Open Source. Open minded' and having over 47,900 GitHub stars.
faqs.question: Is HTTPie CLI open source?

faqs.answer: HTTPie markets 'AI-assisted work' in its Desktop app and has a separate HTTPie AI product, though detailed capabilities are not fully documented on the homepage.
faqs.question: Does HTTPie have AI features?

netdata.name: Netdata
netdata.short_description: Real-time, per-second infrastructure monitoring platform with AI-powered anomaly detection and 800+ zero-configuration integrations.

features.description: Machine learning anomaly detection, automated root cause analysis, and blast radius identification help pinpoint issues faster.
features.title: AI-powered troubleshooting

features.description: Auto-discovery and zero-configuration monitoring across infrastructure, applications, networks, and logs.
features.title: 800+ integrations

features.description: Distributed monitoring keeps raw data on-premises and streams only metadata to the cloud.

features.description: The monitoring agent is designed to run on roughly 5% CPU and 150MB RAM per node.

features.description: Includes topology mapping, NetFlow analysis, and SNMP device monitoring.

features.description: Supports metrics, logs, alerts, and OpenTelemetry data alongside native collectors.

pros: Free Community tier available with no cost for up to 5 connected nodes

Per-node pricing with no usage-based or surprise billing

Business plan is billed per node, which can add up for large fleets
Enterprise On-Premise requires a minimum of 200 node licenses and custom pricing

faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial with unlimited nodes is available for the Business plan.

airbrake.name: Airbrake
airbrake.short_description: Airbrake is an error and performance monitoring platform that detects, groups, and pinpoints application errors in real time.

features.description: Catches application errors with line-of-code precision and groups them intelligently so teams can prioritize fixes.
features.title: Real-Time Error Monitoring

features.description: Lightweight APM tracks HTTP request response times and user satisfaction alongside error data.
features.title: Performance Monitoring

features.description: Correlates errors with deployments to help teams identify which release introduced a problem.
features.title: Deployment Tracking

features.description: Official libraries cover JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Java, Go, PHP, .NET, and other languages.

features.description: Supports SSO for centralized team access to Airbrake accounts.

pros: SDKs available for many popular languages and frameworks

Backed by LogicMonitor for stable ownership and infrastructure
4.7-star rating on G2 based on user reviews
cons: Error monitoring and performance monitoring are billed as separate line items
Entry-level Dev plan is limited to a single user
Full pricing depends on a tiered error-volume structure that requires reading the fine print

faqs.answer: Yes, Airbrake offers a free trial with access to all features, including unlimited errors and unlimited performance monitoring events.

faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $19/month (Dev + Errors) billed monthly, or $17/month billed annually, with higher tiers at $38/month and $76/month.
faqs.question: How much does Airbrake cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, but error monitoring and performance monitoring are billed as separate add-ons.

nagios-xi.name: Nagios XI
nagios-xi.short_description: Enterprise infrastructure monitoring platform with perpetual licensing, event-driven remediation, and full on-premises deployment.

features.description: Personalized tactical dashboards with graphs, gauges, maps, and direct device access via RDP, VNC, or SSH.

features.description: Automatically executes scripts upon problem detection, with configurable escalation policies.

cons: License cost scales quickly with node count, reaching $17,090+ for 1000-node Enterprise
